content.problemStatement:
How do you read a number, say $32510$? \
How big is this number exactly? \
Value of $32510$ is equivalent to $30000+2000+500+10+0$  
which can be simplified as $3 \cdot 10^4 + 2 \cdot 10^3 + 5 \cdot 10^2 + 1 \cdot 10^1 + 0 \cdot 10^0$

This is the value of $32510$ in the decimal number system, where we use $10$ numbers are used as digits. \
$0, 1, 2, ..., 9$ are use to represent one single digit. 

Can we use more or less digits than $10$? \
Yes, in fact all the computers work on a number system with only $2$ digits.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
